text: Politics and political conflict are, in large part, conducted in the currency of words: spoken and written. This is a truism that political scientists have long accepted. Yet the cost of conducting the analysis that politics scientists might wish to do has been prohibitively expensive even for relatively modest corpora of text. Automated text analysis tools promise to greatly reduce this cost, delivering most of the promised benefit. We offer a guide to the new world of political science that these new methods open up, highlighting both their power and their pitfalls. While the tools are innovative and seem to promise great gains, it is essential to remember that these methods are no substitute for hard thinking or close attention to a text and will therefore need to be heavily validated for specific use cases. We summarize many of the approaches and their validity and attempt to correct several errors and misconceptions in the literature and plead for more methods and methodologies of validation in order for the field to develop fully and that the promised methods and tools of the field of political science become a part of the standard arsenal of the political scientist.
explanation: The piece demonstrates domain-specific reasoning and natural scholarly tone with nuanced critique, which is more typical of human authors. There are no obvious AI artifacts or generic phrasing.
